verses.text: Asaph laments that God seems to have cast off His people for ever and asks Him to remember His congregation and visit the ruined sanctuary. The desolation grieves him. The believer mourns when God's house lies in ruins.

Asaph laments that God seems to have cast off His people for ever and asks Him to remember His congregation and visit the ruined sanctuary. The desolation grieves him. The believer mourns when God's house lies in ruins.

Asaph laments that God seems to have cast off His people for ever and asks Him to remember His congregation and visit the ruined sanctuary. The desolation grieves him. The believer mourns when God's house lies in ruins.

Enemies roar in the holy place, hack its carved work, and burn the meeting-places of God throughout the land. Sacred things are violated. The triumph of the ungodly over worship is a deep wound to the faithful.

No signs remain, no prophet speaks, and they ask, 'how long shall the adversary reproach?' urging God not to withhold His hand. Silence from heaven tries them. They plead God to act for His own name.
No signs remain, no prophet speaks, and they ask, 'how long shall the adversary reproach?' urging God not to withhold His hand. Silence from heaven tries them. They plead God to act for His own name.

Yet 'God is my King of old, working salvation,' who divided the sea and crushed the heads of the dragons. Past power feeds present hope. Remembering old deliverances steadies the soul in present ruin.

Day and night, the borders of the earth, summer and winter, all are His handiwork. The whole order of nature is His. The God who governs creation can surely restore His people.
Day and night, the borders of the earth, summer and winter, all are His handiwork. The whole order of nature is His. The God who governs creation can surely restore His people.

He pleads, 'remember' the enemy's reproach and 'deliver not the soul of thy turtledove unto the multitude' of the wicked. He begs mercy for the helpless. God's people, frail as a dove, are precious in His sight.

'Arise, O God, plead thine own cause,' for the tumult of His foes rises continually. He makes God's honour the plea. The wrongs done to God's people are wrongs against God Himself.
